meaning: adj.难以捉摸的,难以寻找的
examples: Andre puts an untraceable cell phone into Jack's pocket and he leaves in the van.
Andre puts an untraceable cell phone into Jack's pocket, and he leaves in the van.
She urgently phones her contact and he orders her to go to Munich and remain untraceable.
Many users of the crypto-currency Bitcoin see it as the first fully anonymous and untraceable currency.
Now it wants to fill its coffers with equally untraceable injections of digital cash.
